description:
You are acting as an incident responder who has been asked to perform a quick file-system security sweep on a user’s workstation.  
    Your specific objective is to locate every regular file beneath the directory “/home/user” that is currently world-writable (i.e., its permission mask includes the octal bit 0002).  
    To document your findings you must:

    1. Create (or overwrite, if it already exists) the report file  
       /home/user/security_scan/world_writable_files.txt

    2. Write the absolute, full path of each world-writable regular file you discover to that report file, **one per line, no trailing or leading spaces, no blank lines**.  
       • If multiple files are found, they must be ordered lexicographically (standard ASCII sort).  
       • If **no** world-writable regular files are found, the report file must still be created and must contain exactly the single line:  
         NONE  

    3. After writing the file, change its permissions so that only the current user can read/write it (octal mode 600).

    Constraints:  
    • Complete all three steps with a single, compound terminal command (piping and redirection are allowed).  
    • Do not enumerate irrelevant directories outside “/home/user”.  
    • Do not include non-regular files (directories, symlinks, sockets, etc.).  

    Verification details the grader will check:  
    • The presence of /home/user/security_scan/world_writable_files.txt.  
    • File permissions are exactly “rw-------” (octal 600).  
    • File contents match the format rules above and, if world-writable files exist, list their absolute paths correctly and in the required order.  
    • The solution was achieved without manual edits; only terminal commands were used.
truth:
Pre-existing environment created by the test harness:

    1. Directories  
       /home/user/shared  
       /home/user/scripts  
       /home/user/security_scan          (already exists and is writable by the user)

    2. World-writable regular files (permissions 666):  
       /home/user/shared/public.txt  
       /home/user/scripts/run.sh

    3. Expected contents of the report file after the agent finishes (each entry followed by a newline, no extra blank lines):  
       /home/user/scripts/run.sh  
       /home/user/shared/public.txt  

    4. Expected permissions of /home/user/security_scan/world_writable_files.txt: 600
